authoritative_sources.title: TIA-942-B Telecommunications Infrastructure Standard for Data Centers
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Authoritative data-center facilities standard defining infrastructure zones, cabling and equipment topology that directly constrain what artifacts this lane must request before execution state movement.
used_for: defining facility record fields that must be checked
terminology normalization for racks, zones, and pathways

authoritative_sources.title: ASHRAE TC 9.9: Thermal Guidelines for Datacom Equipment
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Authoritative thermal/design guidance used in data-center operations to set evidence expectations for cooling, ambient condition, and environmental readiness checkpoints.
used_for: readiness block conditions during completion planning
handling thermal alarm and humidity mismatch scenarios

authoritative_sources.title: Uptime Institute: Tier Standards for Data Center Sites
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Industry framework used for uptime expectation and operational resilience, materially shaping dependency severity, deferred release, and return-to-service prioritization in facilities workflows.
used_for: prioritizing return-to-service when critical services depend on facility state

authoritative_sources.title: NIST SP 800-34 Revision 1: Contingency Planning Guide for IT Systems
authoritative_sources.publisher: National Institute of Standards and Technology (NIST)
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Defines continuity and recovery concepts used to justify safe transition from exception to release with evidence that services can be restored without hidden risk.
used_for: defining what constitutes documented restoration preparedness
escalation when restart criteria are unmet

authoritative_sources.title: NIST SP 800-145: The NIST Definition of Cloud Computing
authoritative_sources.publisher: National Institute of Standards and Technology (NIST)
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Provides baseline service model terminology to keep facility handoff and return decisions aligned with cloud operating context (IaaS/PaaS/SaaS and deployment class distinctions).
used_for: preventing non-facility teams from being silently collapsed

authoritative_sources.title: 29 CFR 1910.147, The Control of Hazardous Energy (Lockout/Tagout)
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Regulatory authority for energy isolation and de-energization, directly limiting what can be claimed as complete or ready-to-return without proper controls in facilities work.
used_for: permit and lockout evidence sufficiency checks
refusal triggers for incomplete de-energization and clearance

workflow_stages.name: Intake and evidence prerequisite capture
workflow_stages.description: Normalize request type, confirm critical artifact IDs (facility ticket, DCIM/BMS records, permit context), and reject unclear requests before any status transitions are suggested.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Execution-state progression and dependency handling
workflow_stages.description: Track readiness milestones against power, cooling, access, labor, permit, and scheduling dependencies, and block progression when any required state condition remains unresolved.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Conflict/escalation and exception management
workflow_stages.description: Handle contradictory telemetry, stale evidence, and policy mismatches by routing exception packets and refusing confident completion when confidence thresholds are not met.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Completion evidence and return-to-service decision
workflow_stages.description: Require synchronized evidence from work-order, environmental, and safety records before return-to-service recommendation, and keep reusable completion artifacts for audit traceability.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

artifacts: Facility intake work-order or change-control record
DCIM rack and electrical feed records
BMS cooling/environment telemetry and alarm history
vendor dispatch, spare, and field resource assignments
critical-load and tenant outage policy matrices
systems_of_record: facility maintenance and field workforce systems
cloud operations reference portal or CMDB

allowed: Assess facility-linked request in-scope versus adjacent authority boundaries
Validate evidence sufficiency for each lifecycle transition
Track dependencies and route blocked/rework outcomes with explicit context
escalate: Missing permit, LOTO, or safety-clearance evidence
Conflicting public and tenant policy on readiness thresholds
Cooling, power, or critical-load risk requiring adjacent safety or change-control specialist
refuse: Marking completion without synchronized closeout evidence
Attempting legal, safety, financial, or engineering-authority decisions
Applying generic completion criteria without tenant policy overrides

domain_failure_modes: Advancing a work item to ready status when DCIM and ticket states disagree
Using generic cloud operation language while ignoring data-center facility dependencies
Assuming permit/lockout closure from one system without retrieval of source-of-truth records
Treating temporary vendor or permit-window delays as non-blockers
Returning service-ready status without evidence of thermal and environmental recovery

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Boundary case that would overgeneralize from the industry name
Request that requires a more specialized adjacent lane
Execution exception that requires rework or escalation
Request to mark work complete without evidence of completion

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Local building code, NFPA, or utility interconnect obligations
unresolved_ambiguities.impact: Jurisdictional obligations can alter permit cadence, required inspections, and release authority by region or tenant contract.
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: Retrieve tenant and local AHJ rules at runtime before status progression and escalate when uncertain.

unresolved_ambiguities.impact: Critical-load classes and service priorities vary by tenant and can materially alter return-to-service deferral thresholds.
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: Require tenant policy retrieval and explicit mapping before final state transitions.

notes: The lane is materially distinct from generic execution functions by requiring DCIM/BMS, permit/LOTO evidence, and critical-load-aware sequencing.
No blocking ambiguity remains for validated operation; unresolved items are explicitly deferred to tenant policy or local authority retrieval.
Every required domain scenario family is mapped to concrete scenario IDs with escalation and refusal behavior anchored to evidence gaps and authority boundaries.
